response: Do not believe the myths that vocational training is lesser than a traditional degree. Do not believe the lies that those who choose hands-on learning are less intelligent or less capable. Do not believe the false narrative that vocational paths are only for those who cannot succeed in academia. Do not believe the slander that those who pursue trade skills are somehow less committed to their future. Do not believe the prejudice that technical training is a shortcut, not a solid foundation. Do not believe the stigma that vocational learners are not as ambitious or as driven as their college-educated peers. Do not believe the false dichotomy that only four-year degrees can lead to success. Do not believe the rhetoric that vocational training is a sign of failure, not a choice of strength. Do not believe the misinformation that those in trades are not respected, not valued, not capable. Do not believe the slander that vocational training is a lesser path, when in reality it is a path of excellence, innovation, and real-world impact. Do not believe the lies that those who choose vocational training are not as smart, not as capable, not as worthy. Do not believe the false claims that vocational learners are not as prepared for the future. Do not believe the slander that vocational training is a dead end, when it is a gateway to opportunity. Do not believe the myths that those who choose trade schools are not as ambitious, not as motivated, not as successful. Do not believe the lies that vocational training is not respected, when it is the backbone of our economy. Do not believe the slander that vocational learners are not as valuable as college graduates. Do not believe the false narrative that vocational training is a second choice, when it is a first choice for many. Do not believe the lies that vocational training is not as respected, when it is the most respected in practice. Do not believe the slander that those who choose vocational training are not as capable, not as skilled, not as dedicated. Do not believe the myths that vocational learners are not as successful, when they are among the most successful in their fields. Do not believe the false claims that vocational training is not a real education, when it is the most practical, the most relevant, the most needed. Do not believe the slander that vocational training is not as valuable, when it is the most valuable in the real world. Do not believe the lies that vocational learners are not as respected, when they are the most respected in their communities. Do not believe the myths that vocational training is not as prestigious, when it is the most prestigious in its own right
